Test data isolation (unique identifiers, cleanup) required for stable E2E suites (grounded: Playwright parallelism docs, QA Wolf).
Playwright CLI is recommended agent-browser interface (~4x token-efficient vs MCP).
Vision-based verification supplements DOM-based testing for layout and visual state.
storageState is the standard pattern for authenticated agent-driven E2E.
neighboringLessonReferencesChanged: m3-l2's risk-tied-input discipline and anti-pattern checklist now explicitly bridged as precedents for m3-l4's seed test and E2E anti-pattern checklist.
m3-l1's multimodal E2E and QA-oriented MCP are operationalized.
m3-l3's pipeline is extended with E2E in CI.
potentialDuplicates: m3-l2 review ritual vs m3-l4 E2E anti-pattern review — boundary clear: same principle, different anti-pattern set for E2E.
m3-l1 risk-to-layer mapping vs m3-l4 pick E2E risks — boundary clear: m3-l1 strategic, m3-l4 operationalizes.

Primary Microsoft source for healer ~75% selector-repair success rate not found.

Whether 10xcards has visual elements suitable for vision-mode demo — needs human decision.

Whether to commit to a specific VLM model recommendation or stay model-agnostic.
Whether Deep Dive includes browser.bind() + page.screencast.
Exact wording of E2E rules template (6-8 rules are provisional).
Whether to cite Debbie O'Brien by name in prose or just link.

groundingSources.title: Playwright CLI Documentation (agent-cli/introduction)
groundingSources.relevance: Primary reference for Playwright CLI: daemon architecture, snapshot-based navigation, element refs, session management, skills system. The CLI is the lesson's primary tool recommendation.
claimsSupported: Playwright CLI is a separate npm package purpose-built for AI coding agents.
Snapshot-based interaction saves YAML to disk; agent reads on demand.
Element targeting via refs, CSS selectors, and role selectors.
Skills system reduces context overhead vs loading full tool schemas.
groundingSources.notes: Verify specific command syntax against latest CLI version at draft time. CLI is actively developed.

groundingSources.title: Playwright for Coding Agents (getting-started-cli)
groundingSources.relevance: Practical setup guide for CLI + agent integration. Documents PLAYWRIGHT_CLI_SESSION env var pattern and CLI vs MCP positioning.
claimsSupported: PLAYWRIGHT_CLI_SESSION=name claude . is the recommended agent integration pattern.
CLI favors token-efficient, skill-based workflows over large tool schemas.
groundingSources.notes: Overlaps with agent-cli/introduction; use the more specific page per claim.

groundingSources.title: Playwright MCP Server (microsoft/playwright-mcp v0.0.75)
groundingSources.relevance: Canonical source for MCP capabilities: snapshot mode vs vision mode, --caps flag system, 30+ tools, network mocking, storage management. The lesson's secondary tool (CLI-first, MCP as comparison).
claimsSupported: Snapshot mode (default) uses accessibility tree; vision mode uses coordinate-based interaction.
Capabilities opt-in via --caps flag: core, vision, pdf, devtools, storage, config, network, testing.
30+ tools covering navigation, form filling, network mocking, storage, tabs, tracing.
groundingSources.notes: v0.0.75 as of 2026-05-26. Pre-1.0 versioning; stable enough for course but API may shift.

groundingSources.title: Playwright Test Agents Documentation
groundingSources.relevance: Official docs for planner/generator/healer agents. Init command, seed test requirement, sequential workflow, agent definitions structure.
claimsSupported: Three agent types: planner (Markdown plans), generator (TypeScript from plans), healer (auto-repair).
Init via npx playwright init-agents --loop=claude (also vscode, opencode).
Requires seed test as execution example.
Agent definitions regenerate on Playwright update.
groundingSources.notes: Healer success rate is NOT documented here. Healer docs on GitHub show no specific success percentages.

groundingSources.title: Playwright Release Notes (v1.56-v1.60)
groundingSources.relevance: Confirms agent-oriented feature evolution: Test Agents (v1.56), browser.bind() + screencast + CLI debugger + trace analysis (v1.59), boxes on aria snapshots + page-level toMatchAriaSnapshot (v1.60).
claimsSupported: browser.bind() enables sharing browser instances between CLI, MCP, and test runner.
page.screencast provides video recording with action annotations.
npx playwright trace enables headless trace analysis for agents.
v1.60 boxes option appends bounding box data to aria snapshots for vision models.
groundingSources.notes: browser.bind() and screencast are Deep Dive candidates. Core lesson uses Test Agents (v1.56) and CLI features.

groundingSources.title: Playwright Authentication Documentation
groundingSources.relevance: Official storageState pattern for authenticated E2E tests. Global setup project, per-test override, security warning about auth files.
claimsSupported: storageState saves cookies, localStorage, IndexedDB to JSON for reuse.
Global setup project runs authentication once; other projects depend on it.
Auth files should be added to .gitignore (security-sensitive).
groundingSources.notes: Standard Playwright pattern. CLI equivalents: state-save/state-load.

groundingSources.title: WebTestBench: Evaluating Computer-Use Agents for Web Testing
groundingSources.relevance: Primary research for the "agent needs your risk map" thesis. Documents the ~2x F1 improvement when agents receive human-written checklists vs autonomous planning. Grounds the hallucinated-assertion failure mode.
claimsSupported: Autonomous E2E testing: all models below 30% F1 (GPT-5.1 best at 26.4%).
With gold checklist: Claude Sonnet 4.5 reaches 49.2% F1 (~2x improvement).
Incomplete checklists are the major bottleneck — agents omit one-third of test cases.
Agents are competent executors but unreliable planners.
groundingSources.notes: Use the ratio (~2x with checklist) rather than exact F1 in lesson prose. Numbers are model-specific and will improve. context/foundation/test-plan.md is the course equivalent of the oracle checklist.

groundingSources.title: VLM Visual Testing: 32 Tests, Zero Dollars (Zak El Fassi)
groundingSources.relevance: Practitioner evidence for VLM-based visual E2E testing: cost analysis (local vs remote), reliability issues (spatial bias, coordinate mismatches), YAML DSL for visual assertions. Grounds the "when NOT to use vision" section.
claimsSupported: VLM-based visual testing is viable with local open-weights models (~4.6s/task, /bin/zsh/run).
Remote API: ~/bin/zsh.009/run, 2.6s/task, rate-limited.
Known reliability issues: spatial bias near viewport edges, Retina coordinate mismatches, prompt precision critical.
Shadow DOM handling: VLM sees rendered result without needing to pierce shadow DOM.
groundingSources.notes: Single practitioner report on one model (Holo3). Do not cite exact pixel numbers. Use as directional evidence for vision limitations. Stay model-agnostic in lesson prose.

groundingSources.title: State of Playwright AI Ecosystem in 2026 (Currents.dev)
groundingSources.relevance: Ecosystem overview with MCP vs CLI token comparison, adoption patterns, and unresolved problems. Frames business logic validation as the core unsolved problem.
claimsSupported: MCP: ~114K tokens per test; CLI: ~27K tokens — ~4x difference.
AI can describe what happened but cannot determine correctness without human-defined intent.
Test governance (explosion of generated tests without prioritization) is a real production problem.
Deterministic baselines first, then layer AI incrementally.
groundingSources.notes: Currents.dev has product interest in test management. Token numbers align with TestCollab analysis. The "introduce incrementally" guidance matches lesson adoption posture.

groundingSources.title: Playwright Test Agents Architecture 2026 (TestQuality)
groundingSources.relevance: Production-ready patterns: storageState injection, token caps, fallback LLM routing, 200-test adoption floor. Names three agent-specific failure modes: hallucinated assertions, tool-loop exhaustion, context-window degradation.
claimsSupported: storageState injection prevents re-authentication loops that waste tokens.
Hard token caps per session essential — teams report hundreds of dollars/month without limits.
Three agent-test failure modes: hallucinated assertions, tool-loop exhaustion, context-window degradation.
Healer achieves ~75% success on selector failures; 25% false-positive rate is real.
Agent-authored tests compress 3-4 hour authoring to 15-20 minutes at ~80-90% locator accuracy.
groundingSources.notes: TestQuality has product interest. The 75% healer number is attributed to "Microsoft benchmarks" without direct link. The failure mode taxonomy is independently useful for lesson structure.

groundingSources.title: Playwright Best Practices (Official)
groundingSources.relevance: Canonical source for E2E quality foundations: locator hierarchy (getByRole first), test independence requirement, web-first assertions over manual waits, auto-waiting. Every rule in the lesson's E2E rules template traces here.
claimsSupported: getByRole is the recommended default locator strategy.
Each test must be completely isolated with its own storage, data, cookies.
Web-first assertions wait until conditions are met; manual waits are anti-pattern.
CSS selectors couple tests to implementation details.
groundingSources.notes: Primary authority for Beat 6 (seed test + E2E rules) and Beat 7 (anti-patterns). All rules derive from official docs.

groundingSources.title: Playwright Test Assertions — waitForTimeout anti-pattern
groundingSources.relevance: Official condemnation of waitForTimeout: 'Never wait for timeout in production. Tests that wait for time are inherently flaky.' Web-first assertions as replacement.
claimsSupported: waitForTimeout is officially designated as an anti-pattern.
Web-first assertions (toBeVisible, toHaveText, toHaveURL) automatically retry until condition is met.
groundingSources.notes: Strongest official source for the waitForTimeout anti-pattern rule.

groundingSources.title: Playwright Test Global Setup and Teardown
groundingSources.relevance: Official teardown project pattern for test data cleanup. Setup→teardown dependency chain in Playwright config.
claimsSupported: Teardown projects run after all dependent projects complete.
testProject.teardown property links setup to cleanup.
groundingSources.notes: Deep Dive source for teardown project pattern.

groundingSources.title: How I Used AI to Fix Our E2E Test Architecture (Debbie O'Brien)
groundingSources.relevance: Strongest practitioner evidence for the lesson's E2E-rules-for-agent thesis. Built a skill with embedded locator priority, anti-pattern detection, fixture scoping. Measured outcomes: 62% setup reduction, 1000 lines removed. 'AI is better at applying known patterns than inventing new ones.'
claimsSupported: E2E rules files for AI agents produce measurable quality improvements.
Locator priority (getByRole first) embedded in agent rules improves test stability.
AI agents detect waitForTimeout, no-op assertions, CSS class selectors as anti-patterns when given rules.
Structured process (rules + skill) beats raw AI capability for E2E test improvement.
groundingSources.notes: Author is on the Playwright team at Microsoft. Not a random practitioner — direct domain authority. Validates lesson's Beat 6 thesis.

groundingSources.title: How to Write E2E Tests for Full Parallelization (QA Wolf)
groundingSources.relevance: Practical patterns for test data isolation: unique identifiers per test, no shared users/sessions, teardown-before-setup approach.
claimsSupported: Each test should create its own record with a unique identifier to avoid collisions.
Tests should never share users, sessions, or pre-seeded records.
Teardown-before-setup guarantees each run starts from a known baseline.
groundingSources.notes: QA Wolf has product interest in parallel testing. Patterns are solid and widely shared.

lessons.title: Debugowanie z AI: od stack trace'a do gotowego fixa
lessons.titleEn: Debugging with AI: from stack trace to a ready fix
owns: Agent-driven diagnostic workflow: ticket → triage → multi-source evidence collection → hypothesis → debug-as-test → verified fix
Structured debug input: parsing a vague user report into actionable diagnostic data with the agent (steps to reproduce, scope, frequency, possible causes)
Debug-as-test as reactive pattern: writing a failing test to confirm a bug (reactive counterpart to m3-l2's proactive test-from-risk — test starts from symptom and works backward to assertion)

Agent as multi-source diagnostician: Sentry MCP (monitoring errors) as primary production source, runtime logs (wrangler tail as platform-specific example) as supplementary signal, Playwright CLI/MCP diagnostic tools (console + network inspection) for local reproduction. The Sentry signal for this swallowed error exists only because the project enables captureConsoleIntegration, which forwards the planted console.warn to Sentry while the handler still returns 200.

Playwright CLI/MCP diagnostic capabilities in debugging context: browser_console_messages, browser_network_requests, browser_network_request — core tools (no --caps flag), reused from m3-l4 in a diagnostic rather than testing role
Triage heuristic: deciding which diagnostic source to tap first based on symptom type

Swallowed-error anti-pattern: API returns 200 but a side effect fails silently — automated gates miss it (the rate/SRS path has no per-commit test at all; unit/integration and the M3L4 E2E both exercise the generate→save flow, not the rate path; SRS-transition correctness is test-plan Risk #6 / Phase 4, not started), so monitoring is the only standing detection signal

Vision-based visual diagnosis as a supplementary debug signal: when a UI bug surfaces during local reproduction and DOM/logs are silent, screenshot + VLM evaluation as a diagnostic question; VLM model categories (frontier/budget/open-weight), cost/latency, and when vision vs deterministic tools — diagnostic use, subordinate to Sentry + Playwright console/network signals, not test generation
referencesOnly: Unit/integration test authoring, risk-tied input + behavioral assertion, anti-patterns (m3-l2 — uses test mechanics for debug-as-test but doesn't re-teach them)
PostToolUse hooks and three-layer quality pipeline (m3-l3 — the pipeline that didn't catch this bug)
E2E test generation, Playwright CLI, seed test (m3-l4 — uses Playwright for reproduction context, doesn't re-teach E2E)
Vision-based verification as a test supplement (m3-l4 — that vision-as-test framing and pixel-regression test tooling live there; here vision is a diagnostic signal, not a test)
Test plan and risk prioritization (m3-l1 — strategic layer; SRS-transition correctness is named as Risk #6 but its rollout phase is not yet built, so the rate path has no standing gate)
MCP servers as agent tools (prework 2.3 — assumes learner knows MCP concept)
Prompt-as-contract patterns (prework 3.2 — structured debug input is analogous)
Context engineering and session lifecycle (prework 3.3 — long debugging sessions)
/10x-new → /10x-plan → /10x-implement as self-setup flow for optional monitoring setup (m2)
mustNotCover: Sentry SDK installation/configuration tutorial (optional in Deep Dive, or self-setup via m2 flow)
Wrangler configuration, deployment, Cloudflare infrastructure (m1-l5, m2-l5)
Unit/integration test authoring from scratch (m3-l2)
Hook configuration or PostToolUse wiring (m3-l3)
E2E test generation or Playwright setup (m3-l4)
Vision-as-test-supplement framing or pixel-regression test tooling (toMatchSnapshot/Argos/Lost Pixel) — that's m3-l4's testing job (mention only, as the deterministic-tools boundary)
Test plan creation or risk prioritization (m3-l1)
Ticket management, backlog triage, PM/QA workflow
Sentry alert rules, source map pipelines, Sentry SDK deep dive
BrowserTools MCP (abandoned project, CVSS 9.8 vulnerability, no unique value over Playwright CLI/MCP — do not mention or recommend)
learningOutcomes: Kursant parsuje vague ticket od użytkownika w structured debug input (kroki reprodukcji, zakres, częstotliwość, możliwe przyczyny) z pomocą agenta.
Kursant kieruje agenta do zebrania danych diagnostycznych z Sentry MCP (issues, stack traces, breadcrumbs) i/lub runtime logs (wrangler tail jako przykład platformowy).
Kursant reprodukuje production buga lokalnie, potwierdzając symptom w kontrolowanym środowisku.
Kursant pisze debug-as-test: integration test, który pada z powodu buga, potwierdza root cause i zostaje jako regression guard po fixie.
Kursant weryfikuje fix across all diagnostic layers: test przechodzi, lokalna reprodukcja działa, monitoring się czyści.
Kursant rozpoznaje swallowed errors (API zwraca 200, ale side effects failują cicho) jako klasę bugów, którą automatyczne bramki przepuszczają, a stałym sygnałem detekcji jest monitoring.
requiredFragments: Stałe rozdziały: Wstęp, Core, Deep Dive, Materiały Dodatkowe.
Ticket od użytkownika jako scenariusz ramowy prowadzący przez cały workflow diagnostyczny.

Celowo wprowadzony bug w 10xCards (swallowed error w src/pages/api/review/[flashcardId]/rate.ts: handler połyka błąd UPDATE na review_states i zwraca 200 z kartą policzoną w pamięci, więc harmonogram nigdy nie zapisuje się i karta wciąż wraca do powtórki) — learner wie, że bug jest zaprojektowany na potrzeby ćwiczenia.
Structured debug input: agent parsuje vague ticket w kroki reprodukcji, zakres, częstotliwość, możliwe przyczyny.
Sentry MCP jako główne narzędzie produkcyjne: search_issues → get_sentry_resource → stack trace → lokalizacja w codebase.

Sentry widzi połknięty błąd tylko dzięki captureConsoleIntegration({ levels: ['warn','error'] }), która przekazuje zaplantowany console.warn do Sentry jako zdarzenie (handler nadal zwraca 200); przechwycone warningi dzielą limit błędów i strumień issues z nieobsłużonymi wyjątkami (darmowy plan 5000/mc), więc przy wzroście ruchu zawęź levels lub użyj captureException.
Runtime logs (wrangler tail) jako uzupełniający sygnał — krótki beat z notą o transferowalności (wrangler dla CF, flyctl dla Fly, vercel logs dla Vercel).
Playwright CLI/MCP diagnostic tools (browser_console_messages, browser_network_requests) przy reprodukcji lokalnej — te same narzędzia z m3-l4, użyte w kontekście diagnostycznym zamiast testowym.
Debug-as-test: integration test, który pada z powodu buga, potwierdza root cause, zostaje jako regression guard. Explicite kontrast z m3-l2: test z buga (reactive) vs test z ryzyka (proactive).
Multi-layer verification: test przechodzi + reprodukcja lokalna OK + monitoring czyści się.
Swallowed-error anti-pattern jako named failure class: 200 OK z failującymi side effects.
Generalizacja: ten sam workflow (evidence → hypothesis → reproduce → test → fix → verify) dla każdego entry point (unit fail, E2E fail, Sentry alert, user ticket).

Vision-as-diagnostic beat: gdy bug wizualny pojawia się przy lokalnej reprodukcji, a DOM i logi milczą — screenshot + VLM jako pytanie diagnostyczne ('co tu jest nie tak?'); kategorie modeli VLM (frontier/budget/open-weight), koszt/latencja, granica vs narzędzia deterministyczne (toMatchSnapshot to robota testowa m3-l4). Sygnał uzupełniający, podrzędny wobec Sentry + Playwright console/network.
Deep Dive: opcjonalny setup Sentry (free tier, @sentry/astro, MCP server auth) z odwołaniem do /10x-new → /10x-plan → /10x-implement flow z m2.
videoPlaceholders: V1 — Full diagnostic walkthrough: ticket → Sentry MCP → wrangler tail → reprodukcja lokalna → code reading → debug-as-test → fix → multi-layer verification. Jeden spójny scenariusz na planted bug w 10xCards.
V2 — (Opcjonalnie) Triage decision demo: 2-3 różne typy symptomów, agent wybiera inne źródło diagnostyczne dla każdego.

newClaimsIntroduced: Debug-as-test as reactive pattern (counterpart to m3-l2's proactive test-from-risk)
Swallowed-error anti-pattern as a named failure class requiring monitoring for detection
Agent as multi-source diagnostician: Sentry MCP as primary production source, runtime logs as secondary platform-specific supplement, Playwright CLI/MCP diagnostic tools for local reproduction
Triage heuristic: symptom type → first diagnostic source
Structured debug input as debugging-domain application of structured input principle (from m3-l2/prework 3.2)
BrowserTools MCP explicitly excluded — abandoned, insecure, no unique capability over Playwright CLI/MCP (verified 2026-05-28)

Sentry surfaces the planted swallowed error only because the project enables captureConsoleIntegration (forwards console.warn to Sentry as an event; handler still returns 200; captured warns share the free-plan errors quota + Issues stream).
claimsRemoved: Retired save-session FK / orphan_review_state bug as the planted example (now covered by 10xCards test-plan Phase 2 save-session.invariants.integration.test.ts).
neighboringLessonReferencesChanged: m3-l2's risk-tied input discipline and test-from-risk now explicitly bridged as proactive counterparts to m3-l5's debug-as-test
m3-l4's Playwright CLI now referenced as reproduction tool in debugging context (not E2E generation)
m3-l1 through m3-l4 collectively referenced as 'the proactive pipeline with blind spots'
potentialDuplicates: Structured debug input (beat 3) echoes m3-l2's structured, risk-tied input principle — same principle, different domain (testing → debugging)
Test writing in debug-as-test (beat 9) uses m3-l2's mechanics — same tool, different reasoning direction (forward from risk vs backward from symptom)
unsupportedFacts: RESOLVED: @sentry/astro + @sentry/cloudflare on Astro 6 is supported via a custom entry point (wrangler main → sentry.server.config.ts wrapping @astrojs/cloudflare/entrypoints/server) per the maintainer comment on issue #19762; 10xCards is Astro 6.3.1 on Workers. Issue #19753 (frontend-only Astro, no adapter) is the separate unresolved case and is out of scope.

RESOLVED: 'will Sentry help if the endpoint returns 200?' — yes, not because Sentry auto-captures console, but because the project opts in to captureConsoleIntegration which forwards the planted console.warn as an event.
Workers Logs ([observability]) works for Workers only, not Pages. 10xCards deploys via Workers — OK for 10xCards, but learners on Pages-only deploys won't have this.
Sentry MCP search_issues LLM requirement RESOLVED — optional, gracefully degrades to direct query pass-through (verified from source).
Sentry MCP get_sentry_resource interface RESOLVED — unified fetcher with URL or explicit mode (verified from source).
Playwright CLI/MCP diagnostic tools VERIFIED (2026-05-28): browser_console_messages, browser_network_requests, browser_network_request are core tools.

Planted bug now in rate.ts (SRS rate handler), signal review/rate: update_failed — VERIFIED against 10xCards source (rate.ts returns 500/404 today; the swallow is introduced by the plant; console.warn string pre-exists; review_states has no CHECK constraints; fetchDueCards selects .lte(due, now); review API + srs.ts have zero tests).

VERIFIED 2026-05-30 (spike): fractional into integer column scheduled_days fails every UPDATE with PG 22P02 (due unchanged); valid write advances due. Escapes all gates — pnpm exec astro check 0 errors, pnpm exec vitest run 50/50 (== clean), lint-staged/eslint 0 errors. Swallow must be type-clean: jsonResponse(200, { ok: true }) (matches save-session precedent), NOT 'updated as RateResponse' which fails TS2352. 10xCards is a pnpm repo with no npm run check script.
videoTextMismatches: Existing video scenario (videos/video-diagnostic-walkthrough.md, commit e9079655) demos the retired save-session bug — must be re-cut for the rate-path bug.
needsHumanDecision: RESOLVED (m3l5-todo-revision): narrative shift to 'Risk #6 / Phase 4 not started, no test touches the rate path' is applied in the draft; E2E framed as covering generate→save (not rate); Sentry honesty via captureConsoleIntegration chosen.

Out of scope (recording-time follow-ups): wire @sentry/astro + @sentry/cloudflare + captureConsoleIntegration into the debug worktree; decide whether the debug-as-test ships as the real first Phase-4 SRS test in 10xCards; re-cut videos/video-diagnostic-walkthrough.md for the rate-path bug.

groundingSources.title: Sentry MCP Server (getsentry/sentry-mcp, v0.35.0)
groundingSources.relevance: Canonical source for Sentry MCP tool surface. CRITICAL: get_issue_details and get_trace_details are now internal primitives behind get_sentry_resource. search_issues requires LLM provider config. Setup flag is --access-token (not --auth-token). Plugin marketplace also available.
claimsSupported: Sentry MCP provides programmatic access to issues, stack traces, breadcrumbs for AI agents.
Free tier supports all data-reading tools except analyze_issue_with_seer (Seer AI, paid only).
search_issues works WITHOUT LLM provider key — gracefully degrades to direct Sentry query pass-through. Learners use Sentry search syntax instead of natural language.
get_sentry_resource is the unified fetcher: URL mode (auto-detect) or explicit mode (resourceType + resourceId + organizationSlug). Returns formatted markdown.
Practical workflow: search_issues → get issue ID → get_sentry_resource(resourceType='issue') → full stack trace.
Hosted endpoint at https://mcp.sentry.dev/mcp available.
groundingSources.notes: Tool surface evolving (pre-1.0). search_issues LLM provider is OPTIONAL (verified from source code handler.ts). get_issue_details is internal behind get_sentry_resource. Lesson should describe capabilities abstractly in Core, show exact 2-step workflow in Deep Dive.

groundingSources.title: Sentry Pricing — Developer (Free) Plan
groundingSources.relevance: Free tier verification: 5K errors/month, 30-day retention, 1 user, API access included. Excludes Seer AI, GitHub/Slack integration, SSO.
claimsSupported: Free tier is sufficient for solo learner projects.
5,000 errors/month and 30-day retention cover the lesson's diagnostic scenario.
MCP and CLI work on free tier (API access included).
groundingSources.notes: Pricing may change. Link to page rather than hardcode numbers in lesson prose.

groundingSources.title: Sentry Astro + Cloudflare SDK Guide
groundingSources.relevance: Setup guide for @sentry/astro + @sentry/cloudflare. Both packages required. Auto-detects Cloudflare adapter since v10.40.0. nodejs_compat flag required. DSN empty = no-op confirmed.
claimsSupported: @sentry/astro + @sentry/cloudflare work together for Astro on Cloudflare.
Empty DSN initializes SDK in no-op mode (graceful degradation).
Source maps upload via both wrangler.toml and Sentry integration config.
groundingSources.notes: WARNING: Astro 6 NOT yet supported (open issue #19753). 10xCards uses Astro 6.3.1 — Deep Dive Sentry setup instructions must note this limitation or check issue status at draft time.

groundingSources.title: Wrangler Pages Deployment Tail — Cloudflare Docs
groundingSources.relevance: Verified command syntax, flags, JSON output structure, and limitations for Pages tail. Confirms --format json, --search, --status filters. Confirms absence of response body and HTTP status code in output.
claimsSupported: wrangler pages deployment tail --format json --search 'text' works for Pages.
JSON output includes outcome, logs[], exceptions[], event.request (url, method, headers, cf).
Response body and HTTP status code NOT available in tail output.
Max 10 concurrent tail clients. Streaming stops above 100 req/s for 5 minutes.
groundingSources.notes: Workers Logs ([observability] in wrangler.toml) free tier: 200K events/day, 3-day retention. However, Pages compatibility not explicitly documented — feature docs reference Workers only.

groundingSources.title: Playwright CLI/MCP Diagnostic Tools (microsoft/playwright-mcp)
groundingSources.relevance: Verified: browser_console_messages, browser_network_requests, browser_network_request are core tools (no --caps flag). CLI equivalents: console, requests, request. BrowserTools MCP confirmed abandoned and insecure — zero unique value.
claimsSupported: Playwright CLI/MCP natively supports console log reading and network request/response inspection.
These are core capabilities — no --caps flag needed.
--caps=network enables route mocking, not reading.
BrowserTools MCP adds zero unique value and is abandoned with CVSS 9.8 vulnerability.
groundingSources.notes: Verified 2026-05-28. Source code analysis of capability tags confirmed core classification.

groundingSources.title: OWASP Top 10:2025 — A10: Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions
groundingSources.relevance: Industry-standard classification of swallowed errors / silent error suppression. New category in 2025 edition. Grounds Beat 11's claim that swallowed errors are a recognized anti-pattern.
claimsSupported: Silent error suppression is classified as a top-10 security/reliability risk (OWASP A10:2025).
'Error hiding' is an established anti-pattern with Wikipedia article and academic study (Padua & Shang, 2019).
groundingSources.notes: Lesson framing is diagnostic (how to detect), not security (how to prevent). Reference OWASP to establish the pattern is named and catalogued.

groundingSources.title: Kent Beck — Test-Driven Development: By Example (2002)
groundingSources.relevance: Canonical source for debug-as-test pattern: when a bug is found, write a failing test that reproduces it, then fix to green. Called 'test-driven bugfixing' in practitioner writing.
claimsSupported: Writing a failing test to confirm a bug before fixing is an established TDD practice, not a novel claim.
The technique is called 'test-driven bugfixing' — the lesson's 'debug-as-test' is a restatement.
groundingSources.notes: The lesson's novel claim is that the agent can drive this reactive workflow, not that the technique itself is new. Acknowledge Beck's precedent.

groundingSources.title: Charity Majors — 'I Test in Production' (InfoQ 2018)
groundingSources.relevance: Strongest practitioner voice for 'monitoring catches what tests miss.' Validates the lesson's thesis: proactive testing catches known risks, observability catches unknown ones.
claimsSupported: 'Tests can't catch what they don't know to look for.'
Observability catches ~80% of shipped bugs before users notice.
Monitoring is complementary to testing, not a replacement.
groundingSources.notes: Talk is from 2018 but argument has only strengthened. Suitable for Materiały dodatkowe. Language: 'testing shows the presence of expected behavior; monitoring shows the presence of unexpected behavior.'

lessons.title: Skalowanie kontekstu dla AI w dużych projektach
lessons.titleEn: Scaling AI Context in Large Projects
owns: ekonomia budżetu uwagi na poziomie architektury plików (rozszerzenie MECW z preworku [3.1]): dlaczego zarówno gruby monolit, jak i przedwczesna struktura marnują kontekst; cztery tryby awarii monolitycznego pliku instrukcji (OpenAI Harness engineering)

progresywna architektura hybrydowa: lean root AGENTS.md jako spis treści/mapa (nie encyklopedia) + context/ jako system-of-record (foundation + changes); podział trójdrożny: konwencje → AGENTS.md, referencje/PRD/plany → context/, procedury → skille
drabina dojrzałości + obserwowalne sygnały eskalacji (root → context/ → per-moduł → governance); reguła decyzyjna = granica deploya/własności, nie liczba folderów

jak realne narzędzia ładują i scalają pliki instrukcji w skali (hierarchia addytywna, nie override; konkatenacja root→cwd; Codex 32KB cap + brak lazy load; Claude Code walk do korzenia filesystemu + lazy subtree; Cursor globy; Copilot applyTo)

kalibracja na realnych repozytoriach: codex (lean root + rzadki liść), cloudflare/workers-sdk (lean root + child index — model wzorcowy), open-mercato (task router — jak wygląda wariant ciężki i jego koszt)
świadomość multi-repo: in-tree nesting kończy się na granicy repo → poziom org/user + dystrybucja + tooling cross-repo; build-out odłożony do M5-L3

throughline certyfikacyjny: architektura kontekstu → raport architektoniczny → odznaka Architekta; pojedyncza aplikacja ⇒ decyzja o architekturze na końcu lekcji lub po ukończeniu modułu (moduły 4–5 nie są wymagane do bazowego certyfikatu Builder)
referencesOnly: autorstwo pojedynczego AGENTS.md / test inkluzji / inner loop / taksonomia reguł-pamięci — m1-l4 (zakładane, operacjonalizowane w skali, nie nauczane od nowa)
Write/Select/Compress/Isolate — prework [3.3] (nazwane jako teoria, którą się operacjonalizuje)
mechanika skilli 10x-workflow (/10x-init, /10x-prd, /10x-agents-md, /10x-rule-review, /10x-lesson) — używana jako wehikuł demo, nauczana wcześniej w m1-l1/m1-l4
Team Shared AI Registry / infrastruktura dystrybucji — M5-L3 (wskazane, nie budowane)
pętle samoutrzymania/governance — wzmiankowane lekko (/10x-rule-review, /10x-lesson); pełny governance to temat pracy/Track-B+
mustNotCover: ponowne nauczanie autorstwa single-project AGENTS.md / testu inkluzji / inner loop (właściciel: m1-l4)
workflow legacy, które teraz NASTĘPUJĄ: mapowanie kodu, analiza feature, refaktoryzacja, ekstrakcja domen DDD (m4-l2…m4-l5 po renumber) — fundament ich nie zakłada ani nie wyprzedza
budowa zespołowego Shared AI Registry / CLI / dystrybucji paczek (M5-L3)
głęboka mechanika MCP / budowa serwerów MCP (nazwane tylko jako opcja cross-repo w polyrepo)
wybór modeli / benchmarki (prework [3.5])
learningOutcomes: Kursant wyjaśnia, dlaczego pojedynczy gruby AGENTS.md / zrzut kontekstu degraduje wyniki agenta w skali (skończony budżet uwagi → context rot; cztery tryby awarii monolitu) i czym różni się to od single-project z m1-l4.
Kursant scaffolduje bazową skalowalną architekturę: lean, referencujący root AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md + context/ jako system-of-record (foundation + changes), używając 10x-workflow.

Kursant stosuje drabinę dojrzałości: nazywa, na którym szczeblu jest jego projekt i obserwowalny sygnał, który uzasadnia awans (root > ~200–300 linii; moduł zyskuje własny deploy/owner; agent wielokrotnie gubi ten sam kontekst modułu).

Kursant opisuje, jak jego narzędzie realnie ładuje i scala pliki instrukcji (hierarchia addytywna, nie override; root→cwd; Codex 32KB cap + brak lazy load; Claude Code walk do korzenia filesystemu + lazy subtree; Cursor globy; Copilot applyTo).

Kursant jest świadomy, że multi-repo (polyrepo) łamie in-tree nesting i wymaga instrukcji na poziomie org/user + dystrybucji + toolingu cross-repo — a build-out zespołowy to M5-L3, potrzebny tylko firmom multi-repo.
Kursant tworzy sekcję „architektura kontekstu” swojego raportu architektonicznego (wybrany szczebel, scaffold, sygnały eskalacji, uzasadnienie).
requiredFragments: Cztery tryby awarii monolitycznego pliku instrukcji (OpenAI Harness): wypycha zadanie, rozcieńcza wskazówki, gnije, utrudnia weryfikację.
Podział trójdrożny: konwencje → root AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md, referencje/PRD/plany → context/, procedury → skille.
Diagram before/after: gruby monolit (źle) vs lean root + context/ (lepiej).
Diagram dwuwarstwowej architektury (lean root → context/ foundation+changes → skille) z what-goes-where.
Demo na 10xCards: 82-liniowy lean CLAUDE.md (Project/Commands/Architecture/Conventions/Reference) + context/foundation + context/changes + .claude/skills.
Narracja dual-MVP: 10xCards = przykład roboczy, projekt kursanta z modułu 1 = miejsce zastosowania (jawny handoff w beatach demo i kalibracji).
Drabina dojrzałości + obserwowalne sygnały eskalacji; reguła decyzyjna = granica deploya/własności, nie liczba folderów; progi jako heurystyki.
Diagram drabiny z sygnałami eskalacji między szczeblami.
Kalibracja na realnych repo: cloudflare/workers-sdk (child-index wzorzec), open-mercato (ciężki), openai/codex (minimalny) + pułapka liczenia przez GitHub search.
Kontrast ładowania w Deep Dive: Claude Code (walk do korzenia FS + lazy subtree) vs Codex (eager root→cwd, wspólny limit rozmiaru, brak lazy load); hierarchia addytywna, nie override.
Multi-repo jako świadomość: polyrepo łamie in-tree nesting → org/user + dystrybucja + cross-repo (--add-dir/MCP); build-out = M5-L3.
Zadanie obowiązkowe: zaprojektuj i zescaffolduj architekturę kontekstu swojego projektu + napisz sekcję architektura kontekstu raportu architektonicznego.

Throughline certyfikacyjny: Builder (CRUD + 1 logika biznesowa + testy oparte o ryzyko) vs ścieżka Architekta; moduły 4–5 nie są wymagane do bazowego Builder; pojedyncza aplikacja => decyzja na końcu lekcji/modułu.
videoPlaceholders: Prowadzący odpala /10x-init na projekcie kursowym i pokazuje scaffold context/ + lean AGENTS.md, który go referencuje; kontrast grubego AGENTS.md vs lean root + context/.
Prowadzący demonstruje decyzję o eskalacji: rosnący root → nazwanie sygnału (własny deploy/owner) → wydzielenie jednego modułu do osobnego AGENTS.md + child index (wzorzec cloudflare).
(Opcjonalnie) side-by-side tego samego lean roota jako CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md / .cursor/rules w różnych narzędziach.

groundingSources.title: On the Impact of AGENTS.md Files on the Efficiency of AI Coding Agents
groundingSources.publisherOrAuthor: Lulla, Mohsenimofidi, Galster, Zhang, Baltes, Treude
groundingSources.relevance: Empirical efficiency side of beat 2: in the measured setup a lean AGENTS.md was associated with ~28.64% lower median runtime and ~16.58% lower output-token use (124 PRs, 10 repos). Already verified for m1-l4.
claimsSupported: In one small-PR study, AGENTS.md was associated with lower median agent runtime and lower output-token use.
Use the ~29% runtime / ~17% output-token figures only with Codex/small-PR caveats.
Supports an efficiency argument, not a broad correctness or all-agent guarantee.
groundingSources.notes: v2 last revised 2026-03-30. Keep phrasing scoped: 'w badaniu na OpenAI Codex, dla małych PR-ów'. This is the source of the -28% TIME figure (distinct from 2602.11988's +20% COST).

groundingSources.title: Evaluating AGENTS.md: Are Repository-Level Context Files Helpful for Coding Agents?
groundingSources.relevance: Counterweight for noisy/encyclopedia files (beat 2 'fat monolith is measurably worse' + the inclusion-test discipline): redundant or unnecessary context can reduce success while increasing inference cost by over 20%. Already verified for m1-l4.
claimsSupported: Redundant or unnecessary context files can increase inference cost by over 20%.
Context files can encourage broader exploration and stronger obedience to instructions.
Human-written context files should describe minimal requirements.
groundingSources.notes: This is the source of the +20% COST figure. The causal warning is redundancy/unnecessary requirements, not raw file length. progressive-adoption.md mis-attributed BOTH '-28% time / +20% cost' to this single ID — the -28% is actually 2601.20404.

groundingSources.title: Effective context engineering for AI agents
groundingSources.relevance: Primary anchor for the finite attention-budget thesis and just-in-time/hybrid context as the scaling answer.
claimsSupported: context window is a finite resource with diminishing returns (attention budget)
JIT/hybrid context: hold lightweight identifiers, load at runtime via tools
Claude Code as exemplar: CLAUDE.md up front, glob/grep retrieve just-in-time
groundingSources.notes: Corroborated by context-rot and Lost in the Middle; do NOT quote a hard token threshold (model/task-specific).

groundingSources.title: Harness engineering
groundingSources.relevance: Strongest on-thesis primary source: lean AGENTS.md as table-of-contents/map -> docs system-of-record; the four failure modes of a monolithic instruction file (verbatim).
claimsSupported: table of contents not encyclopedia; ~100-line map + progressive disclosure
structured docs/ as the system of record
four failure modes: crowds out the task, dilutes guidance, rots, resists verification
groundingSources.notes: Describes a separate internal Codex-built product; public openai/codex root AGENTS.md is a 286-line Rust-scoped file (say-do gap to teach honestly).

groundingSources.title: Claude Code — Manage Claude's memory
groundingSources.relevance: Primary for Claude Code load/merge mechanics (beat 7), maintenance loop (beat 5), and polyrepo (beat 8).
claimsSupported: filesystem-root walk, concatenate ancestors root->cwd (never override)
lazy on-demand subtree loading; four scopes; claudeMdExcludes
@path imports expanded at launch, 4-hop recursion, do NOT save tokens (Claude-Code-specific)
auto memory (default-on) distinct from human-written CLAUDE.md; --add-dir for cross-repo
groundingSources.notes: Live-proof of the subdirectory-launch rule observable in workbench sessions.

groundingSources.title: Claude Code — Manage large codebases
groundingSources.relevance: Why single-root fails (fat-vs-generic dilemma) and why per-directory layering needs ownership (governance collapse).
claimsSupported: single root file grows to cover every subsystem or stays too generic
per-directory layering stops scaling: conventions drift, files go stale, no one owns the root
<200 lines per file target
groundingSources.notes: <200-line number is a vendor target, not a measured threshold.

groundingSources.title: Claude Code best practices
groundingSources.relevance: Fat-monolith failure made concrete + the maintenance discipline behind /10x-rule-review. Inclusion test is reference-only (m1-l4 owns teaching it).
claimsSupported: bloated CLAUDE.md causes Claude to ignore actual instructions (lost in the noise)
per-line inclusion test; exclude frequently-changing info; ruthless pruning; periodic audit
groundingSources.notes: Vendor best-practice/opinion, not a measured study.

groundingSources.title: Agent Skills (engineering post + platform overview)
groundingSources.relevance: Three-tier progressive disclosure: the procedures->skills arm of the three-way split and the only verified way to break the more-modules=more-context curve.
claimsSupported: L1 name+description ~100 tokens always-on; L2 full SKILL.md <5k when matched; L3+ bundled resources unbounded
many Skills installable with no upfront context penalty
groundingSources.notes: JIT defers cost, does not erase it; see also platform.claude.com/docs/en/agents-and-tools/agent-skills/overview.

groundingSources.title: OpenAI Codex — AGENTS.md guide + config reference
groundingSources.relevance: The Claude-Code-vs-Codex sharp contrast (beat 7): the most demonstrable 'your tool doesn't load this the way you think' moment.
claimsSupported: eager additive merge root->cwd, one file/dir, built once at launch, no lazy load, not re-read mid-session
project_doc_max_bytes 32 KiB combined cap, silent truncation (may be 64 KiB on newer builds)
root-detection walks up to git root, then loading walks down; AGENTS.override.md hard-replaces at same level
groundingSources.notes: Two originally-circulating claims were refuted: Codex does NOT walk up to merge; the cap is combined, not per-file. Cap value is version-sensitive.

groundingSources.title: agents.md open spec + v1.1 proposal (#135)
groundingSources.relevance: Same filename != same semantics: spec was ambiguous (nearest wins) until v1.1 'local extends ancestor' (Jan 2026, not yet merged).
claimsSupported: open spec only said 'nearest file takes precedence' (merge-vs-replace ambiguous)
v1.1 #135: a local AGENTS.md extends ancestor files rather than replacing
AGENTS.md as cross-tool single-file mitigation (24 tools, 60k+ repos)
groundingSources.notes: v1.1 not yet merged; say 'proposed'. Among popular tools only Claude Code does not read AGENTS.md.

groundingSources.title: GitHub Copilot — custom instructions (IDE/CLI) + organization instructions
groundingSources.relevance: Additive merge across a third tool (beat 7) and uneven org-wide support (beat 8).
claimsSupported: merges, never selects: path-specific + repo-wide both apply; applyTo globs
org custom instructions apply only to Chat/code-review/cloud-agent on GitHub.com, NOT IDE or CLI
groundingSources.notes: Nested precedence is intended but has surface gaps; conflict resolution explicitly non-deterministic.

groundingSources.title: Cursor — Rules
groundingSources.relevance: Four rule types map onto the task-vs-module split; glob-scoped rules as the 'topic not directory' exemplar (twenty).
claimsSupported: Always Apply (global), Apply Intelligently (by task), Apply to Specific Files (globs, by module), Apply Manually (@-mention)
groundingSources.notes: Apply Intelligently has the same non-determinism as Skills (model must choose).

groundingSources.title: Claude Code — sub-agents + MCP
groundingSources.relevance: Read-only reviewer/auditor sub-agent as the maintenance pattern (beat 5); MCP scopes for cross-repo runtime visibility (beat 8).
claimsSupported: canonical first sub-agent example is a read-only reviewer (no Edit/Write)
no vendor doc names a sub-agent dedicated to instruction-file auditing -> course contribution
MCP scopes: project .mcp.json shared, user ~/.claude.json cross-project, managed, plugin-bundled
groundingSources.notes: Sub-agent persistent memory maintains its own store, not CLAUDE.md; see also code.claude.com/docs/en/mcp.

groundingSources.title: Nx configure-ai-agents (single-source generation)
groundingSources.relevance: Single-source generation kills cross-tool drift (beat 8 pointer to M5-L3); Materialy dodatkowe.
claimsSupported: generate CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/Cursor/Copilot/Codex from one source to kill cross-tool drift
groundingSources.notes: Solves multi-tool drift in one repo; multi-repo drift still needs cross-repo sync. 'Same capabilities' is a design statement, not an audited result.

groundingSources.title: Ruler — single-source fan-out to 30+ agent tools
groundingSources.relevance: Per-repo single source fanned out across tools' native files; the distinction multi-tool vs multi-repo drift (beat 8 / M5-L3).
claimsSupported: one .ruler/ source -> fan-out to CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, Cursor, Copilot, Codex native files
groundingSources.notes: Solves multi-tool drift inside one repo only; syncing the source across repos is still your responsibility.

groundingSources.title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
groundingSources.relevance: Academic grounding for self-updating learnings loops (agents improve via linguistic feedback, not weight updates).
claimsSupported: language agents improve through linguistic feedback rather than updating weights
groundingSources.notes: Episodic buffer, not rule-file edits; bridge to CLAUDE.md maintenance is analogical, not literal.

groundingSources.title: Real-repo calibration — 5 repos cloned at HEAD (internal evidence)
groundingSources.relevance: Calibration / 'what good looks like at scale' (beats 5-6) + the escalation-trigger table. Direct disk inspection of openai/codex, mastra, cloudflare/workers-sdk, open-mercato, twenty.
claimsSupported: instruction-file counts are strategy-dependent (2/16/8/32), not a target
cloudflare/workers-sdk lean-root + child index + 'see root' leaf = the copyable model
codex: 121 crates but 1 nested file -> per-crate co-location is NOT the convention
count by cloning not GitHub search; '88 files' is OpenAI internal monorepo not public repo
groundingSources.notes: Structure observed directly; effectiveness NOT measured. Single HEAD snapshot 2026-06-02, counts will drift.

unsupportedFacts: Maturity-ladder thresholds (~200-300-line root, own-deploy/owner trigger) are heuristics from vendor targets + repo case studies, NOT measured.

The arXiv productivity figures are Codex/small-PR-scoped and directional: -28% median runtime / -16% output tokens from arXiv 2601.20404, and >20% inference-cost increase from redundant context from arXiv 2602.11988 (both verified for m1-l4). Do NOT present as universal across agents/tasks; progressive-adoption.md mis-attributed both numbers to a single ID.
Effectiveness/ROI of any instruction-file maintenance mechanism (pruning, auto-memory, claude-reflect, generators) is undocumented; existence is documented, outcomes are not.
CODEOWNERS + CI review gates for instruction files are NOT vendor-documented; team convention only.
Codex project_doc_max_bytes default is version-sensitive (32 vs 64 KiB); version-check before stating a number.
needsHumanDecision: Certification-form wording + architectural-report template confirmation against the official Builder and Architect & Champion forms (Marcin refined the framing in positioning-and-certification.md — moduly 4-5 not required for the base Builder badge; architecture decided at end of lesson or after the module — but the official-form wording/template is still unconfirmed).

lessons.title: Agent w projekcie legacy - generowanie Mapy projektu
lessons.titleEn: Agent in a Legacy Project: Generating the Project Map
owns: Mapa projektu ① jako finalny artefakt Wide Scan zapisywany w `context/map/repo-map.md`: krótkie sekcje, dowody z komend, jawne `unknowns` i lista miejsc, w których trzeba uważać przed większą zmianą.

Trzy robocze artefakty skanu w `context/map/` (`artifact-1-territory.md`, `artifact-2-structure.md`, `artifact-3-contributors.md`) jako notatki z kolejnych sesji, syntetyzowane dopiero na końcu do `context/map/repo-map.md` (nazewnictwo przykładowe, do dostosowania przez kursanta).
Wide Scan -> Deep Focus jako sekwencja wejścia w obce legacy: M4-L2 wykonuje szeroki, płytki skan wrażliwości repo; M4-L3 może pogłębić jeden z wrażliwych obszarów.
Full repo dump jako antywzorzec startowy: duże okno kontekstu nie oznacza dobrego zrozumienia architektury ani priorytetów.
Foldery jako hipoteza, nie architektura: drzewko katalogów pokazuje położenie plików, ale nie ważność, aktywność, sprzężenie, entry pointy ani kontekst zespołowy.
Trzy składowe Mapy projektu: Terytorium (gdzie projekt żyje i boli), Struktura (jak zmiana może się rozlać), Kontekst kontrybutorów (gdzie kod ma pamięć poza kodem).

CLI jako compact evidence dla agenta: `git`, `rg`/`find`, structural search i grafy zależności (`dependency-cruiser` jako narzędzie wiodące dla JS/TS, z `madge`/`skott` jako lżejszymi wariantami) skanują repo poza oknem kontekstowym, a agent syntetyzuje skondensowany wynik.
Operacyjne wnioski po każdej składowej mapy: `evidence / inference / caution / unknowns`, żeby wynik nie był dumpem danych z narzędzi.

Dowodowa klasyfikacja modułów: core/supporting/peripheral, deep/shallow, stable/volatile/seasonal, load-bearing/contained, high/medium/low sensitivity oraz contract/implementation layer, jeśli taki podział widać.
Mapa kontrybutorów jako część legacy onboarding: historia commitów pogrupowana po autorach i tematach wskazuje, do czyich PR-ów i decyzji warto zajrzeć przed większą zmianą.
Manual sanity checks dla Mapy projektu: realność entry pointów, konfiguracja dependency toolingu, path aliases, workspace config, cykle i jawne oznaczanie luk jako `unknown`.
referencesOnly: M4-L1 context architecture: lean root, `context/`, maturity ladder i zarządzanie uwagą — L2 korzysta z tej mentalności, ale jej nie uczy od nowa.
Prework [3.1] i [3.3]: ograniczone okno kontekstu i `Select` — L2 operacjonalizuje je w legacy.
M1-L4 agent onboarding: mapa może później zasilić instrukcje projektu, ale lekcja nie jest tutorialem pisania AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md.
M4-L3 Deep Focus: feature/data-flow analysis, hotspoty, szczegółowy risk read, change coupling i decyzje modernizacyjne.

Cztery rodziny wyszukiwania repo, dwa znaczenia semantic search i klasyfikacja modułów to materiał Deep Dive; kompaktowa tabela odpowiedników narzędzi per stack (variadic mapping) żyje w sekcji strukturalnej jako materiał wspierający, nie główny cel lekcji.
mustNotCover: ponowne nauczanie AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md, inclusion testu, inner loop ani architektury `context/`
pełna analiza feature'a, przepływ danych end-to-end, luki testowe i decyzja 'co zmodernizować' — właściciel: m4-l3
behavioral code analysis jako metoda: hotspot = complexity x churn, change coupling, temporal coupling i sum-of-coupling — właściciel: m4-l3
refaktoryzacja, plan zmian, safety net i weryfikacja refaktoru — właściciel: m4-l4
DDD, bounded contexts, ekstrakcja domen i modernizacja legacy — właściciel: m4-l5
szczegółowy, krok-po-kroku tutorial instalacji każdego narzędzia CLI (kompaktowa tabela odpowiedników per stack jest dozwolona jako materiał wspierający)
twierdzenie, że narzędzia automatycznie rozumieją architekturę; narzędzia produkują sygnały, a interpretacja należy do agenta i człowieka
learner-facing strong claims o niezweryfikowanych narzędziach, wersjach, licencjach, capability claims lub akademickich metrykach bez dodatkowego grounding
learningOutcomes: Kursant wyjaśnia, dlaczego pełny zrzut repo do LLM-a jest złą strategią startową: nie mieści się w praktycznym budżecie kontekstu albo daje streszczenie bez priorytetów.
Kursant stosuje proces Wide Scan -> Deep Focus i wie, że M4-L2 kończy się Wide Scan, a M4-L3 może pogłębić jeden z wrażliwych obszarów.

Kursant tworzy Mapę projektu ① w `context/map/repo-map.md` (po drodze trzy robocze artefakty `artifact-1/2/3`) z krótkimi sekcjami, dowodami z komend, jawnie oznaczonymi `unknowns` i listą miejsc, w których trzeba uważać przed większą zmianą.
Kursant odróżnia drzewko katalogów od mapy projektu: foldery są hipotezą, a architektura wymaga sygnałów o zależnościach, entry pointach, historii zmian i kontekście zespołowym.
Kursant zbiera trzy składowe mapy: Terytorium, Struktura i Kontekst kontrybutorów.
Kursant zapisuje po każdej składowej operacyjne `evidence / inference / caution / unknowns`, zamiast zostawiać surowe rankingi narzędzi.
Kursant używa agenta jako tłumacza między pytaniem w języku naturalnym a przewidywalnym CLI, a nie jako wyroczni, która rozumie repo bez dowodów.
Kursant ręcznie weryfikuje mapę: sprawdza entry pointy, konfigurację grafu zależności, aliasy, cykle i miejsca, gdzie agent powinien powiedzieć `unknown`.
requiredFragments: Bridge z M4-L1: własny rosnący projekt vs obce albo zapomniane legacy.
Antywzorzec startowy: 'agent, przeczytaj całe repo i wyjaśnij architekturę' oraz dlaczego duże okno kontekstu nie rozwiązuje tego problemu.
Definicja procesu Wide Scan -> Deep Focus: L2 robi mapę wrażliwości legacy, L3 może zrobić głęboką analizę wybranego obszaru.

Definicja Mapy projektu i ścieżki `context/map/repo-map.md`, plus trzy robocze artefakty `context/map/artifact-{1-territory,2-structure,3-contributors}.md` syntetyzowane na końcu (nazewnictwo przykładowe).
Sekcja 'Czego nie mówi drzewko katalogów': foldery jako hipoteza, nie architektura.
Sekcja 'Efektywna Mapa projektu': kluczowe moduły, częstotliwość zmian, obszary wrażliwe, coupling, blast radius, kontrybutorzy i `evidence / inference / caution / unknowns`.
Pętla język naturalny -> agent -> CLI -> compact evidence -> synteza -> zawężenie.
Trzy składowe Mapy projektu: Terytorium, Struktura, Kontekst kontrybutorów.
Mattermost jako główny przykład: duże, realne, sprawdzalne repo legacy-style z backendem Go i frontendem React/TypeScript.
Mapa terytorialna Mattermost: aktywne katalogi, trendy kwartalne, korytarze zmian oraz `Sensitive areas from change history`.

Mapa strukturalna Mattermost z `dependency-cruiser` dla `webapp`: cykle w aktywnych obszarach, granice warstw (`platform/types` -> `platform/client` -> `channels`), ryzyka testowalności, taming zbyt gęstego grafu (`--collapse`/`--focus`/`--reaches`/`--exclude node_modules`) oraz kompaktowa tabela odpowiedników per stack; wynik jako `Structural sensitivities`.
Mapa kontrybutorów Mattermost: kontrybutorzy, tematy commitów i `Contributor/context risks` dla wybranego korytarza zmian.

Prompt syntezy `context/map/repo-map.md` z trzech roboczych artefaktów (bez generowania danych od zera, bez kopiowania ich tabel w całości) o strukturze: TL;DR z Mermaid głównych warstw, Teren, Realne powiązania (couplingi + warstwy + cykle), Strefy ryzyka (4-6 obszarów), Kogo zapytać, Pierwszy dzień (5-8 plików), Ograniczenia; z zakazem proponowania refaktoryzacji i wymaganiem `unknowns`.
Strefy ryzyka w `repo-map.md` (4-6 obszarów wysokiego ryzyka z jedną linijką 'dlaczego') jako miejsca do sprawdzenia dowodów, testów, PR-ów i zależności przed większą zmianą.
Ćwiczenie praktyczne (Krok 0-4): w `context/map/` zbuduj trzy robocze artefakty, a następnie zsyntetyzuj `context/map/repo-map.md` dla repo, którego nie znasz dobrze, z ręcznymi sanity checks.
Deep Dive: cztery rodziny wyszukiwania repo, granice każdej składowej, dwa znaczenia semantic search oraz jak klasyfikować moduły na mapie (klasyfikacja przeniesiona z głównej osi do Deep Dive).
videoPlaceholders: Wide Scan na repozytorium Mattermost: historia gita jako mapa terytorialna, `dependency-cruiser` jako mapa strukturalna, analiza kontrybutorów i tematów commitów jako mapa kontrybutorów; po każdym kroku zapis do roboczego artefaktu w `context/map/` z `evidence / inference / caution / unknowns`, a na końcu synteza `context/map/repo-map.md` ze Strefami ryzyka.

groundingSources.title: dependency-cruiser
groundingSources.relevance: JS/TS dependency-graph tool that validates against custom rules and detects cycles; produces the structural skeleton of the Mapa projektu artifact.
claimsSupported: Validates dependencies against rules and detects circular dependencies (default rule set)
Emits agent-consumable JSON, DOT, Mermaid, CSV, HTML, text, d2, metrics
Language coverage limited to JavaScript/TypeScript/CoffeeScript (incl. JSX/TSX, Vue, Svelte)
MIT, Node/npm CLI, cross-platform, actively maintained (v17.4.3, May 2026)
groundingSources.notes: Verified 3-0 against primary repo. Mermaid output ties into house Mermaid workflow. JS-family only — pair with jdeps/goda for the variadic angle.

groundingSources.title: madge
groundingSources.relevance: Fast 'show me the cycles' dependency-graph CLI for JS; the cheapest structural map signal.
claimsSupported: Generates module-dependency graph, finds circular dependencies (rendered red), detects orphan/leaf modules
Output: --json (pipeable), --dot; --image requires a system Graphviz install
Coverage: JS (AMD/CommonJS/ES6) + CSS preprocessors; MIT; v8.0.0
groundingSources.notes: Verified 3-0 (repo + npm). JSON/DOT are the agent-ingestible outputs; only --image needs Graphviz (a real setup gotcha to flag).

groundingSources.title: skott
groundingSources.relevance: Modern JS/TS module/dependency analyzer with first-class TypeScript path-alias handling; alternative to madge for real TS monorepos.
claimsSupported: Deep circular-dependency detection with configurable max search depth (digraph-js)
Coverage: .js/.jsx/.cjs/.mjs/.ts/.tsx, ESM + CommonJS, TypeScript path aliases
Output: JSON, Mermaid, SVG/PNG/MD (static needs @skottorg/static-file-plugin), web UI, terminal tree; MIT; v0.35.9 (Mar 2026)
groundingSources.notes: Verified 3-0. Optional — don't over-stack tools. Static-file output needs the extra plugin.

groundingSources.title: jdeps (JDK dependency analyzer)
groundingSources.relevance: Variadic proof point (Java): the Mapa projektu workflow works on a non-JS stack with a tool that ships in the JDK.
claimsSupported: Shows package-level or class-level dependencies of Java class files
-dotoutput emits one .dot per archive + summary.dot of inter-archive dependencies (Graphviz/agent-readable)
JDK-bundled since JDK 8, GPLv2+CE, cross-platform
groundingSources.notes: Verified 3-0. Static analysis only — misses reflection/Class.forName/service-loading; state this limit.

groundingSources.title: goda (Go dependency analysis toolkit)
groundingSources.relevance: Variadic proof point (Go): module-clustered dependency graphs for the territory map.
claimsSupported: DOT output piped to Graphviz, Go-template-customizable node formatting
Module-level clustered graphs (goda graph -cluster -short) and package specifiers + set arithmetic (:mod, :all, :import)
MIT; v0.9.2 (Feb 2026); Go-only
groundingSources.notes: Verified 3-0. -cluster groups related packages, not strictly Go-module boundaries — don't overstate as automatic module detection.

groundingSources.title: ast-grep
groundingSources.relevance: Flagship 'offload structural retrieval to a deterministic tool' example: agent runs AST search and reasons over compact --json instead of reading files.
claimsSupported: Rust CLI for structural code search/lint/rewrite over tree-sitter ASTs (matches AST nodes, not text; $MATCH wildcards, AST types, regex)
Multi-threaded, many languages, --json output; MIT; v0.43.0 (May 2026)
Installable via npm/pip/cargo/homebrew/scoop/MacPorts
An official ast-grep Agent Skill exists exposing the tool to AI agents
groundingSources.notes: Verified 3-0 (repo + docs/tool-comparison). The agent-tooling pattern is vendor-endorsed here.

groundingSources.title: Probe — AI-friendly code search engine
groundingSources.relevance: Search tool built for agents: returns complete functions/classes (not text lines) the agent can reason over.
claimsSupported: Combines ripgrep speed with tree-sitter AST parsing; returns complete functions/classes
Output: markdown/json/xml; Apache-2.0; v0.6.0-rc323 (Jun 2026)
groundingSources.notes: Verified 3-0. CRITICAL: Probe's 'semantic' = AST-structural, NOT embeddings/RAG. Do not cite as an embedding-RAG example (the lesson lists semantic/RAG as a separate family).

groundingSources.title: Universal Ctags
groundingSources.relevance: The cheap, classic symbol index for the structural/symbol search family — 'where is X defined' without an LSP server.
claimsSupported: Maintained ctags implementation; generates a project-wide tag index of language objects/symbols
GPL-2.0, cross-platform; v6.2.1 (Oct 2025)
groundingSources.notes: Verified 3-0.

groundingSources.title: SCIP — SCIP Code Intelligence Protocol
groundingSources.relevance: Code-as-data endpoint of the structural/symbol family: precomputed go-to-def/find-refs data an agent queries instead of reading files. Update 'SCIP/LSIF' in owns — SCIP superseded LSIF.
claimsSupported: Language-agnostic, Protobuf-based code-indexing format powering Go-to-definition / Find-references / Find-implementations
Superseded LSIF (LSIF read support removed in Sourcegraph 4.6)
groundingSources.notes: Verified 3-0 (repo + announce blog). DO NOT cite a specific count/roster of SCIP indexers — that claim was refuted (1-2). Keep to 'language-agnostic; indexers exist for several languages'.

newClaimsIntroduced: Draft-backed schema contract: lesson spine is Mattermost-based Wide Scan that produces Mapa projektu ①.
Mapa projektu is now framed as a legacy sensitivity map: key, active, load-bearing and risky areas before bigger changes.
Mapa projektu has three owned components: Terytorium, Struktura and Kontekst kontrybutorów, each producing evidence/inference/caution/unknowns rather than raw tool dumps.
Kontekst kontrybutorów is part of the map and uses contributor/topic history as a pointer to human/PR context before bigger changes.
The final map ends with risk zones (Strefy ryzyka), not primarily a Deep Focus shortlist.

Artifact layout is `context/map/`: three working scan artifacts (artifact-1-territory, artifact-2-structure, artifact-3-contributors) synthesized at the end into `context/map/repo-map.md` — not a single `context/repo-map.md`.

dependency-cruiser is the featured structural tool (cycles, layer boundaries, test-risk, density-taming flags); madge/skott are lighter alternatives and a compact per-stack table covers other ecosystems.
Evidence-based module classification is taught in Deep Dive (`Jak klasyfikować moduły na mapie`), not on the main spine; the main structural section carries the variadic per-stack table.
Tool taxonomy and semantic-search nuance are supporting Deep Dive material, not the main lesson spine.
claimsRemoved: Academic metric formulas and architecture lenses as core learner-facing ownership for this lesson.
Broad 'core five' CLI-tool bundle as a required main-path toolset.
Repo-search taxonomy as a central learning outcome instead of supporting Deep Dive material.
Deep Focus shortlist as the primary output of M4-L2.
neighboringLessonReferencesChanged: M4-L1 remains bridge-in through attention/context discipline only; M4-L2 does not re-teach context architecture.
M4-L3 can consume one sensitive area from the map, but M4-L2 itself is complete as a sensitivity map.

RESOLVED (propagated this pass per user decision): M4-L3's contract and spec now use `context/map/repo-map.md` and the Polish `Strefy ryzyka` section. This matches both the M4-L2 draft and the M4-L3 draft, which already used the new path/section name; only the M4-L3 contract+spec had lagged. Module bridge is now fully consistent.
potentialDuplicates: M4-L1 context scaling — controlled by bridge only; no re-teaching `context/`.
M4-L3 behavioral analysis — controlled by explicit boundary: L2 maps, L3 analyzes feature/risk.
M1-L4 agent onboarding — controlled by not turning map output into AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md tutorial.
unsupportedFacts: Exact Mattermost line-count and numeric examples should be verified before RC if they were not produced by a recorded grounding/demo run.
Tokenizer/context-window claims should stay tied to current supporting sources in `Materiały dodatkowe`.
Specific tool capabilities should remain grounded in the listed source material and not be expanded without verification.
needsHumanDecision: Final confirmation that Mattermost is the canonical demo repo.
Whether to run a targeted grounding/QA pass for Mattermost numbers before RC.
RESOLVED: propagated the new `context/map/repo-map.md` path and `Strefy ryzyka` naming to M4-L3's contract and spec; the M4-L3 draft already used them, so the whole module bridge is now consistent.

The draft's Materiały dodatkowe now cite three token/context-window sources (OpenAI Help Center, Claude support article, arXiv tokenizer paper 2601.11518) that are not in `groundingSources`; a grounding pass should verify them before adding.

lessons.title: Analiza feature z AI: co działa, co kuleje, co zmodernizować
lessons.titleEn: Feature Analysis with AI: What Works, What Falls Short, What to Modernize
owns: artefakty z Mapy projektu ① jako wejście do Deep Focus: `Strefy ryzyka`, wybrany wrażliwy obszar, etykiety modułów, entry pointy, korytarz zależności, kontekst kontrybutorów, unknowns i ślady dowodów z komend
kontrakt zakresu dla agentowego Deep Focus: jeden feature/moduł, jawne granice czytania, dowody przed interpretacją, zakaz losowego deep-read i zakaz refaktoryzacji przed M4-L4

`/10x-research` w trybie kontrolowanym: znany greenfield research skill nakarmiony mapą jako priorem i kontraktem zakresu; jedno zapytanie rozbite na dokładnie trzy sub-agenty (trace e2e, luki w testach, blast radius = graf statyczny + co-change z gita); wynik do `context/changes/<feature-slug>/research.md` przez `/10x-init`, `/10x-new`

hotspoty jako iloczyn złożoności i częstości zmian — behavioral code analysis priorytetyzująca ryzyko na podstawie danych, nie przeczuć (A. Tornhill, Your Code as a Crime Scene / Software Design X-Rays)
connascence jako precyzyjna taksonomia siły i lokalności sprzężenia (statyczna: name/type/meaning/position/algorithm; dynamiczna: execution/timing/value/identity) (M. Page-Jones)
change coupling / temporal coupling: moduły zmieniające się razem w historii są sprzężone niezależnie od zależności statycznych (Gall i in.; 'sum of coupling' wg Tornhilla)

strukturalna konfirmacja CLI: podział pracy agent (znaczenie/zachowanie) vs deterministyczne CLI (liczba/pozycja); AST vs tekst; `ast-grep` jako structural matcher z placeholderami (`Save` vs `SaveMultiple`); reguła 'licz ast-grepem, każde zero potwierdzaj grepem'; tryb rewrite (`-r`, `--interactive`, `-U`, reguła YAML z `fix`, `ast-grep scan`) jako zapowiedź M4-L4, w L3 nieużywany
dwa elementy raportu z jednej analizy modułu: ② przepływ danych end-to-end (feature overview) oraz ③ technical debt (ryzyko, blast radius, luki testowe)
referencesOnly: M4-L1 context architecture: `context/` jako trwały dom artefaktów i zarządzanie budżetem uwagi — L3 korzysta z tej infrastruktury, ale jej nie uczy od nowa.
M4-L2 Mapa projektu: terytorium, struktura, kontekst kontrybutorów, deep/shallow, core/supporting/peripheral i mapa zależności są wejściem do L3, nie materiałem do ponownego nauczania.
M4-L4 refactor opportunities: L3 może nazwać ryzyko, coupling, blast radius i luki testowe, ale nie wybiera target architecture ani strategii migracji.
M4-L5 DDD opportunities: L3 może zauważyć napięcia językowe/domenowe jako ryzyko, ale nie prowadzi Event Stormingu ani nie projektuje bounded contexts.
mustNotCover: ponowne tworzenie Mapy projektu, teoria whole-repo mapping, deep/shallow modules, static dependency graphs i klasyfikacja modułów — właściciel: m4-l2
refactor strategies, target archetypes, Strangler Fig, Branch by Abstraction, Mikado Method, characterization tests i plan zmiany — właściciel: m4-l4
DDD, Event Storming, ubiquitous language, bounded contexts, agregaty i value objects — właściciel: m4-l5

tool tutorial dla CodeScene, code-maat, lizard albo raw git pipelines; narzędzia mają ilustrować metodę, nie stać się tematem lekcji (wyjątek: `ast-grep` to backbone konfirmacji w L3 i dostaje sensowne pokrycie instalacji oraz trybu read/rewrite, wciąż jako metoda, nie wyczerpujący tool reference)
twarde cytowanie niezweryfikowanych atrybucji Page-Jones/Weiser oraz przedstawianie agentowego diagramu przepływu jako prawdy bez weryfikacji w kodzie
learningOutcomes: Kursant wybiera jeden cel Deep Focus na podstawie artefaktów z `context/map/repo-map.md`: sekcji `Strefy ryzyka`, etykiet modułów, entry pointów, korytarzy zależności, unknowns i kontekstu kontrybutorów.
Kursant zamienia artefakty z repo mapy w kontrakt pracy dla agenta: zakres, dozwolone źródła, hipotezy do sprawdzenia, dowody do zebrania i zakaz refaktoryzacji.

Kursant uruchamia `/10x-research` w trybie kontrolowanym: jedno zapytanie karmione mapą jako priorem rozbija na dokładnie trzy sub-agenty (trace e2e, luki w testach, blast radius = graf statyczny + co-change), a wynik zapisuje do `context/changes/<feature-slug>/research.md`.
Kursant tworzy Feature overview ②: end-to-end przepływ danych przez wybrany moduł/feature z diagramem oraz oznaczeniem evidence/inference/unknown.
Kursant tworzy Technical debt ③: hotspoty, change coupling, connascence, blast radius, luki testowe i unknowns ugruntowane w danych, nie w przeczuciach.

Kursant potwierdza twierdzenia strukturalne raportu narzędziem `ast-grep` (wzorce w składni języka, `Save` vs `SaveMultiple`), a każde zero potwierdza grepem; wie, że ten sam wzorzec działa też w trybie rewrite (zapowiedź M4-L4), z którego w L3 jeszcze nie korzysta.
Kursant odróżnia Deep Focus od losowego deep-read: agent czyta wąsko, według mapy, z dowodami i z zatrzymaniem przed M4-L4.
requiredFragments: Bridge z M4-L2: Mapa projektu ① kończy się sekcją `Strefy ryzyka`, a L3 zaczyna od wyboru jednego wrażliwego obszaru do Deep Focus.

Sekcja pokazująca, które artefakty z `context/map/repo-map.md` są wejściem do L3: `Strefy ryzyka`, etykiety modułów, entry pointy, korytarz zależności, kontekst kontrybutorów, unknowns i ślady dowodów.
Antywzorzec: `agent, przeanalizuj ten moduł` bez repo-map input bundle, bez scope guard i bez rozróżnienia evidence/inference/unknown.

Prompt/kontrakt dla agenta w trybie Deep Focus przez `/10x-research`: jedno zapytanie rozbite na dokładnie trzy sub-agenty (trace e2e, luki w testach, blast radius = graf statyczny + co-change), jawne granice, weryfikacja w kodzie/testach/komendach, brak refaktoryzacji, zapis do `context/changes/<feature-slug>/research.md`.
Feature overview ②: przepływ input -> command/handler -> reguły -> state changes -> side effects -> output jako diagram i krótki opis.
Technical debt ③: hotspot lens, change coupling, connascence labels, blast radius, luki testowe i unknowns.

Konfirmacja strukturalna `ast-grep`: agent (znaczenie) vs CLI (liczba/pozycja), AST vs tekst, wzorce z placeholderami (`Save` vs `SaveMultiple`), prompt-śledztwo potwierdzające/obalające twierdzenia strukturalne raportu, reguła 'każde zero potwierdzaj grepem', oraz zapowiedź trybu rewrite jako mostu do M4-L4 (NIE używanego w L3).
Granica do M4-L4: L3 kończy się dowodami i ryzykiem; M4-L4 dopiero zamienia je w refactor opportunities.
videoPlaceholders: Demo: prowadzący bierze `context/map/repo-map.md` z M4-L2 i wybiera jeden wrażliwy obszar do Deep Focus na podstawie `Strefy ryzyka`, etykiet, entry pointów i unknowns.

Demo: prowadzący buduje kontrolowane zapytanie `/10x-research` z input bundle mapy, rozbite na trzy sub-agenty (trace / luki w testach / blast radius), i zapisuje wynik do `context/changes/<feature-slug>/research.md`.
Demo: agent buduje Feature overview ②, a prowadzący weryfikuje diagram z kodem oraz oznacza evidence/inference/unknown.
Demo: hotspot/change-coupling pass dla wybranego celu i zapis Technical debt ③ bez proponowania refaktoryzacji.
Demo: prowadzący bierze twierdzenie strukturalne z raportu, buduje wzorzec `ast-grep` (`Save` vs `SaveMultiple`), potwierdza/obala je, a zero potwierdza grepem.

groundingSources.title: Your Code as a Crime Scene (1st ed. ~2015, 2nd ed. 2024) & Software Design X-Rays (2018)
groundingSources.relevance: Origin of the hotspot lens: intersect code complexity with change frequency mined from version-control history; the evidence engine for report element ③.
claimsSupported: Hotspots = complexity × change frequency, both from VCS history
Prioritize code that is both frequently modified and hard to understand
Change/temporal coupling framed as co-changing files (X-Rays 'Coupling in Time')
groundingSources.notes: Adversarially verified (3-0). 1st-ed date ambiguous (2014 publisher vs 2015 paperback) — write '1st ed. ~2015, 2nd ed. 2024'.

groundingSources.title: indent-complexity-proxy
groundingSources.relevance: Confirms Tornhill's complexity proxy is indentation/size-based and language-neutral — NOT cyclomatic. Corrects a common misconception.
claimsSupported: Complexity proxied by source-code indentation patterns, not cyclomatic complexity
Indentation correlates medium-to-high with McCabe cyclomatic and Halstead (Hindle, Godfrey & Holt, ICPC 2008)
groundingSources.notes: Adversarially verified (3-0). CodeScene docs corroborate indentation-based complexity over cyclomatic.

groundingSources.title: code-maat (sum_of_coupling, logical_coupling) + CodeScene
groundingSources.relevance: Open-source tooling that mines git history for hotspots and change/temporal/logical coupling; CodeScene is the commercial successor. Operationalizes the L3 lenses.
claimsSupported: code-maat mines VCS history; accompanies Tornhill's two books
Logical coupling = modules that tend to change together (hidden, implicit dependency)
'Sum of coupling' (SoC) ranks each module's overall co-change involvement
groundingSources.notes: Adversarially verified (3-0). Do not conflate SoC (per-module ranking) with code-maat's separate pairwise 'coupling' analysis.

groundingSources.title: Detection of Logical Coupling Based on Product Release History
groundingSources.publisherOrAuthor: H. Gall, K. Hajek, M. Jazayeri (TU Vienna) / IEEE ICSM 1998, pp. 190-198
groundingSources.relevance: Primary source for change/temporal/logical coupling: elements that co-change during evolution are coupled regardless of static dependencies — surfaces blast radius the L2 dependency graph cannot see.
claimsSupported: Logical coupling = observed identical change behavior of elements during system evolution
Detects coupling otherwise hidden in the source code
groundingSources.notes: Adversarially verified (3-0). Venue/authors corroborated via DBLP + BibBase + PDF body.

groundingSources.title: Use of Relative Code Churn Measures to Predict System Defect Density
groundingSources.publisherOrAuthor: N. Nagappan, T. Ball / ACM ICSE 2005, pp. 284-292
groundingSources.relevance: Empirical basis for churn as a risk signal — but RELATIVE churn (normalized by size/time), not absolute. Optional depth behind element ③.
claimsSupported: Absolute churn is a poor defect predictor; relative churn is highly predictive of defect density
groundingSources.notes: Adversarially verified (3-0). Grounded in one industrial case study (Windows Server 2003) — strong result, not a universal law.

groundingSources.title: A Complexity Measure
groundingSources.publisherOrAuthor: T. J. McCabe / IEEE TSE, SE-2(4), Dec 1976, pp. 308-320
groundingSources.relevance: Defines cyclomatic complexity — one complexity proxy. Useful when contrasting with Tornhill's indentation proxy (control-flow vs size).
claimsSupported: Cyclomatic complexity v(G) = e - n + p; counts max linearly independent paths
groundingSources.notes: Adversarially verified (3-0). Control-flow (not data-flow) measure.

groundingSources.title: Cognitive Complexity (white paper + ACM TechDebt 2018)
groundingSources.relevance: A human-understandability complexity proxy distinct from cyclomatic — optional alternative metric for the hotspot complexity dimension.
claimsSupported: Cognitive Complexity measures relative understandability, combining cyclomatic precedents with human assessment
Distinct from cyclomatic complexity
groundingSources.notes: Concept verified (3-0). DATE CORRECTION: 2018 origin (ACM TechDebt 2018), 2021 is a republish — not a 2021 metric. Attribute as 'Sonar's metric' (widely reimplemented).

groundingSources.title: Connascence taxonomy (connascence.io, Wikipedia, practitioner write-ups)
groundingSources.publisherOrAuthor: Community (concept popularized via M. Page-Jones / Jim Weirich)
groundingSources.relevance: Precise coupling vocabulary (static: name/type/meaning/position/algorithm; dynamic: execution/timing/value/identity; stronger+more-distant = higher priority) to replace 'tightly coupled' in element ③.
claimsSupported: Static and dynamic connascence taxonomy
Stronger + more distant connascence = higher refactor priority (degree/locality/strength)
groundingSources.notes: Concept well-attested across sources; PRIMARY attribution to Page-Jones (~1995/1996; UML book 1999) NOT yet adversarially verified — confirm before a hard citation in prose.

groundingSources.title: Making Legacy Code AI-Ready / Agentic Refactoring (CodeScene blog)
groundingSources.relevance: Existence-proof that hotspot/behavioral analysis + AI agents is a live, productized workflow — framing reference for the AI-as-ally angle.
claimsSupported: Behavioral code analysis is being combined with AI agents for refactoring (2025)
groundingSources.notes: Vendor source with commercial interest; benchmark numbers self-reported and time-sensitive. Use for framing, not hard metrics. NOT adversarially verified.

newClaimsIntroduced: Artefakty z Mapy projektu ① są formalnym wejściem do M4-L3 Deep Focus: `Strefy ryzyka`, etykiety modułów, entry pointy, korytarz zależności, kontekst kontrybutorów, unknowns i ślady dowodów.
Draft settles L3 as owner of both report elements: ② Feature overview and ③ Technical debt (③ renamed from 'Issues & hot spots' — canonical name is Technical debt).

Refinement after real React and Mattermost repo maps: L3 must convert a sensitivity warning into one flow, because learner maps may contain `Executive Read`, `Strefy ryzyka`, load-bearing contracts and unknowns rather than a clean shortlist.

Back-prop from draft (draft = SoT): analysis engine is `/10x-research` in controlled mode — one query decomposed into exactly three sub-agents (trace e2e, test gaps, blast radius = static graph + git co-change) via `/10x-init`, `/10x-new`, output to `context/changes/<feature-slug>/research.md`.
