lessons.title: Innovate: Async & Remote Agents - deleguj i zajmij się czymś innym
lessons.titleEn: Innovate: Async & Remote Agents – delegate and get on with something else
owns: Control-moment model for async/remote agent work: real-time remote control, kick-off-and-monitor, scheduled/recurring review
Three remote-execution archetypes as a task-driven choice: SSH/tmux baseline, Happy local-compute phone control, managed cloud sandbox
Transferable managed-cloud sandbox configuration model: setup script, network policy, MCP/config, cache, secrets, pre-installed tool assumptions
Isolation as autonomy enabler: filesystem and network restrictions make unattended work safer instead of merely slowing the agent down
Delegate-and-monitor-from-phone workflow for bounded tasks, ending in explicit review criteria
`/loop` and routines as scheduled or recurring control modes with review-time responsibility and failure awareness
Failure mode: control did not disappear; green run, cost, security, access gates and review risk moved into setup and review boundaries
referencesOnly: Ralph technique and `/goal` delegation as ancestor/back-pointer material already owned by m2-l5
git worktrees, independent slice selection and parallel-at-desk workflow owned by m2-l5
multi-agent orchestrator roundup (Cursor Agents, Antigravity, Superset, Conductor, Claude Code Agent View, VS Code Agents) owned by m2-l5
managed-agents SDK/API as the programmatic face of sandboxing; SDK-building depth owned by m5-l2
Cloudflare Agents / Workflows as an optional durable async runtime path for teams building their own agent loop; not a Core remote-coding sandbox
Shared AI artifacts and committed `.mcp.json` as distribution concepts owned by m5-l4
Prework [2.1], [2.4], [3.3] and [1.2] as assumed vocabulary, not repeated theory
mustNotCover: Teaching git worktree mechanics or independent-slice selection
Re-teaching Ralph or `/goal` from scratch
Building an agent from an SDK or implementing managed-agents API flows
Building a Cloudflare Agents app, Durable Object architecture or Workflows implementation tutorial
Repeating the m2-l5 product/orchestrator survey
Review standards and Definition of Done depth from m5-l3
Beginner cloud/IaC tutorial
Asserting first-party Anthropic Remote Control mobile mode unless official docs verify it before drafting
Exact product tiers, pricing, resource limits, installed tool lists or cache windows without pre-draft verification
learningOutcomes: Learner can compare SSH/tmux, Happy and managed cloud sandboxes by control moment, compute location, trust surface, setup cost and review point.
Learner can configure a managed cloud sandbox using a transferable setup/network/MCP/cache/secrets model before starting a bounded task.
Learner can explain why filesystem and network isolation enable safer unattended autonomy.
Learner can delegate a bounded task, monitor or steer it from a phone when useful, and review output against explicit criteria.
Learner can use `/loop` or routines as scheduled/recurring control modes while recognizing green-run, cost, access-gate and review failure modes.

groundingSources.title: Happy (Happy Coder) — mobile/web client for Claude Code & Codex
groundingSources.relevance: Remote/mobile control of a LOCAL agent — run `happy`/`happy codex`, take over from phone, one keypress back to desktop; agent runs locally, cloud is only an E2E-encrypted relay
claimsSupported: Open-source MIT mobile+web client wrapping Claude Code and Codex; agent executes on your own machine, not in the cloud
Phone↔computer connect via a central relay (not P2P/SSH) with end-to-end encryption; QR-shared key the relay never sees; explicitly avoids SSH port-forwarding
groundingSources.notes: Third-party, not first-party. Frequently mis-described as a cloud agent — it is not. CLI wrapper is sibling repo slopus/happy-cli. Architecture docs at https://happy.engineering/docs/how-it-works/

groundingSources.title: Claude Code Remote Control (continue a local session from any device)
groundingSources.relevance: First-party counterpart to Happy for Tryb 1: drive a LOCAL Claude Code session from the Claude mobile app or claude.ai/code; execution stays on your machine, web/mobile is only a window. Leads Tryb 1 and reframes Happy as the third-party fallback.
claimsSupported: Real, shipped Anthropic feature (research preview, launched 25 Feb 2026); commands `claude remote-control` (server mode), `claude --remote-control`/`--rc`, and in-session `/remote-control`/`/rc`; `/mobile` shows an app-download QR
Local execution — nothing moves to cloud; outbound HTTPS only, no inbound ports; survives laptop sleep/network drop and auto-reconnects; mobile push notifications (v2.1.110+)
Plan-gated to Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise (NOT API keys); requires Claude Code v2.1.51+; Team/Enterprise off until an admin enables the toggle
groundingSources.notes: CORRECTS the prior grounding error: the 2026-06-09 pass recorded Remote Control as REFUTED from a secondary VentureBeat report; the official docs page now verifies it, satisfying the mustNotCover 'unless official docs verify it' clause. Research preview — re-verify version/plan gates before publishing. Docs also map the three lesson modes: Remote Control (Tryb 1), Claude Code on the web (Tryb 2), Scheduled tasks/routines (Tryb 3), plus Dispatch and Channels.

groundingSources.title: Claude Code on the web (cloud sandbox)
groundingSources.relevance: Primary 'true async delegation' path: fresh managed VM with repo cloned, persists when laptop closed, monitor from Claude mobile app; the concrete configure-libraries/CLIs/MCP payload
claimsSupported: Each `--remote` session = fresh Anthropic-managed VM, repo cloned, persists across browser close, parallel sessions, `--teleport` pulls one back; no separate compute charge; ~4 vCPU/16GB/30GB
Pre-installed runtimes (Python/Node/Ruby/PHP/Java/Go/Rust/Docker/Postgres16/Redis7) but gh NOT pre-installed; 4 network levels (None/Trusted-default/Full/Custom) via security proxy
Root Bash setup script on Ubuntu 24.04, snapshot-cached (~7d); MCP loads ONLY from repo .mcp.json, not from local `claude mcp add`
groundingSources.notes: RESEARCH PREVIEW, plan-gated (Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise). Tool list is Anthropic-specific, not Codex. Re-verify installed-tools list against live docs at draft time — most likely field to drift.

groundingSources.title: Anthropic managed-agents API — environments
groundingSources.relevance: Programmatic/API face of the same sandbox model; likely referencesOnly depth (overlaps m5-l2 SDK lesson)
claimsSupported: Each session = isolated fresh Linux container; sessions may share an environment but not filesystem state
`packages` field pre-installs across apt/cargo/gem/go/npm/pip with pinning; network unrestricted (default) or limited(allowed_hosts + opt-in MCP/registry toggles)
groundingSources.notes: Beta API. Keep shallow unless spec consciously owns the SDK path — overlaps m5-l2.

groundingSources.title: OpenAI Codex Cloud — environments, internet access, MCP
groundingSources.relevance: Codex side of the cloud-sandbox comparison; the 'internet off during agent phase, on during setup' asymmetry is the best concrete sandbox gotcha
claimsSupported: Default `universal` image (openai/codex-universal, Ubuntu 24.04); internet OFF by default during agent phase, ON during setup scripts; proxy on all outbound; container cache up to 12h
Internet has TWO states (Off/On); On adds allowlist (None/Common-deps/All) + optional GET/HEAD/OPTIONS restriction; MCP via `codex mcp add` and `codex mcp login` (OAuth)
groundingSources.notes: CORRECTION: two access states, not three (earlier phrasing conflated states with the three allowlist presets). Codex universal toolset != Anthropic's list — don't blend. internet-access doc: https://developers.openai.com/codex/cloud/internet-access ; MCP doc: https://developers.openai.com/codex/mcp

groundingSources.title: Claude Code sandboxing (security rationale)
groundingSources.relevance: Security spine: why both filesystem AND network isolation matter; the link between sandboxing and unattended autonomy
claimsSupported: Effective sandboxing needs BOTH filesystem and network isolation; without network isolation a compromised agent could exfiltrate SSH keys; without filesystem isolation it could escape the sandbox
Sandboxing reduces permission prompts ~84% (enabling more autonomous unattended work)
groundingSources.notes: 84% is a VENDOR SELF-REPORT, undisclosed methodology — attribute as Anthropic internal testing, do not validate. Open-sourced runtime: github.com/anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime (bubblewrap/seatbelt)

groundingSources.title: Claude Code routines (scheduled autonomous cloud loops)
groundingSources.relevance: First-party productized 'loop' — the safe, verified answer to autonomous loops that does NOT require re-teaching Ralph; recommended anchor for the loop angle
claimsSupported: Routines run autonomously on Anthropic cloud as full CC sessions, no permission prompts during a run, keep running when laptop closed
Three triggers — Scheduled (cron, min 1h)/API (HTTP POST + bearer)/GitHub events; combinable in one routine; Default env Trusted network, other hosts 403 host_not_allowed
groundingSources.notes: RESEARCH PREVIEW. Green run status = session exited without infra error, NOT task success — key caveat for unattended trust.

groundingSources.title: The Ralph technique / loop pattern — Geoffrey Huntley
groundingSources.publisherOrAuthor: Geoffrey Huntley (built CURSED this way; SF teaching; '$50k for $297' case study)
groundingSources.relevance: Names the X-trending pattern and its origin; conceptual ancestor of the productized loop commands. m2-l5 ALREADY links this — cite once, do not re-teach
claimsSupported: Ralph in pure form = `while :; do cat PROMPT.md | claude-code ; done`; one task per loop; memory on disk/git not conversation; fresh context per iteration
Author's own caveats: not for existing codebases ('no way in heck'); 'you'll wake up to a broken codebase'; engineers still required
groundingSources.notes: OVERLAPS m2-l5 (which already links ghuntley.com/ralph). 'Everything is a Ralph loop' (17 Jan 2026, ghuntley.com/loop) reframes looping as a mindset shift. Use only as ancestor → point to first-party tools.

groundingSources.title: Productized loop commands — snarktank/ralph, /loop, Codex /goal
groundingSources.relevance: Concrete loop implementations + the parts m2-l5 does NOT own: Claude Code `/loop` (self-pacing/recurring) is the strongest unique hook
claimsSupported: snarktank/ralph: fresh instance per iteration; state via git history + progress.txt + prd.json passes flags; emits <promise>COMPLETE</promise>, default max 10 iterations (the safeguards raw bash lacks)

Claude Code `/loop`: recurring interval (`/loop 5m /foo`) or omit-interval to self-pace; loop-aware; --interval(min 1m)/--cron, ~3-day max. Codex got built-in Ralph loop in CLI 0.128.0 (30 Apr 2026, Brockman: 'built in Ralph loop++') via /goal
groundingSources.notes: `/loop` + snarktank mechanics high; Codex-/goal framing MEDIUM (ralphable.com is SEO — verify version/date against OpenAI changelog). m2-l5 ALREADY teaches `/goal`; `/loop` is NOT yet referenced by m2-l5 → unique to m5-l5.

groundingSources.title: SSH + tmux / mosh remote-to-a-box path
groundingSources.relevance: The 'you already own this, zero new vendor' remote-control baseline; pairs with Happy (low-setup) and cloud (managed) as three archetypes
claimsSupported: SSH/mosh into a dev box/VPS, run the agent inside tmux (survives disconnect), reconnect from a phone terminal (Termius/Blink), ntfy for push when the agent needs you
groundingSources.notes: Well-established practitioner pattern but NO surviving primary-source verification this pass — present as a known pattern, not a sourced spec. Twingate variant: https://www.twingate.com/blog/claude-code-termius-tmux

groundingSources.title: Cloudflare Agents / Workflows — durable async agent runtime
groundingSources.relevance: Deep Dive / references-only option for teams building a durable async agent runtime: durable identity/state, local SQL, scheduling, recovery, Workflows, MCP/tools, and loop-connected background work
claimsSupported: Cloudflare Agents provide durable agent identity, local SQL state, sessions/routing, WebSockets, scheduling, recovery/observability, and tools including MCP/browser/sandbox-oriented capabilities
Long-running agents are durable identities rather than always-on processes; state, SQL data, schedules and recovery checkpoints survive hibernation/restarts
Workflows complement Agents for durable multi-step background processing, automatic retries/recovery, waiting for external events and human approval flows
groundingSources.notes: Added after user request on 2026-06-13. Keep referencesOnly/Deep Dive: useful platform path for building your own durable async agent runtime, not a Core remote-coding sandbox like Claude Code on the web or Codex Cloud.

newClaimsIntroduced: Final ownership boundary: m5-l5 owns the control-moment model, three remote-execution archetypes, transferable cloud-sandbox configuration, isolation-as-autonomy, delegate-and-monitor workflow, `/loop`/routines as scheduled control, and review-time failure modes
Cloudflare Agents / Workflows are now a references-only Deep Dive option for durable async agent runtimes, backed by official Cloudflare docs checked 2026-06-13

Tryb 1 now leads with first-party Claude Code Remote Control (verified via official docs 2026-06-14): local execution, `claude remote-control` / `/rc`, Claude mobile app + claude.ai/code, no inbound ports, research preview on Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise, v2.1.51+. Happy is reframed as the third-party fallback for API-key/open-source/Codex users; SSH/tmux stays the zero-vendor baseline; VPS-vs-local-machine tradeoff added
